title: Crispy Vegan Portuguese Tripe Stir-fry
description: This dish is a fresh and crispy twist on traditional Portuguese tripe, made vegan-friendly with plant-based alternatives.

ingredients.name: Vegan Tripe
ingredients.notes: Ensure it is fully cooked and cleaned.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Chopped finely for the best flavor integration.

ingredients.name: Garlic Cloves
ingredients.notes: Finely minced to add depth of flavor.

ingredients.name: Tomato Paste
ingredients.notes: For a sweet and tangy base.

ingredients.name: Cumin Powder
ingredients.notes: To give the dish a smoky, earthy flavor.

ingredients.name: Red Pepper Flakes
ingredients.notes: To add a touch of heat.

ingredients.name: Water or Vegetable Stock
ingredients.notes: For boiling and deglazing the pan.

instructions: Boil the Vegan Tripe in water for at least 30 minutes, changing the water a couple times to remove any impurities.
Drain and set aside. Heat olive oil in a wok or large frying pan over medium heat.
Add finely chopped onions and minced garlic, sautéing until translucent.
Pour in tomato paste and let it cook for about 1 minute, stirring continuously.
Return the boiled tripe to the pan with water/stock. Add cumin powder and red pepper flakes.
Stir well to coat all pieces evenly, allowing any excess liquid to evaporate slightly.
Season with salt and pepper according to taste.
Sauté for another 5 minutes or until the mixture starts to crust on top and forms a golden-brown layer.
